Please include:

  • Portfolio link

  • Hourly rate

  • WhatsApp number

Email: office@annatambini.com

apiJob.title: Mid-Weight Digital Graphic Designer
apiJob.description:
Job Title: Mid-Weight Digital Graphic Designer

Location: United Kingdom (Remote-first with in-person meetups and occasional travel)

Company: Haus of Apps (part of the VentureHaus Group)

Employment Type: Full-time

Annual Salary Range: £35,000 - £40,000 (depending on experience)

About Us

Haus of Apps is a global development company with operations across the United Kingdom, USA, and Dubai. We specialise in building innovative mobile applications, SaaS platforms, and AI-powered digital products that shape the future of how people connect, work, and create.

Haus of Apps is part of the VentureHaus Group — a fast-growing multi-business portfolio spanning marketing, technology, consultancy, and eCommerce. The group brings together dynamic teams, visionary leadership, and a culture of innovation to build and scale modern businesses globally. Working with us means being part of an ecosystem that values creativity, collaboration, and excellence across every project.

The Role

We’re seeking a motivated and ambitious Mid-Weight Digital Graphic Designer to join our growing product and design team.This role is perfect for someone with strong design fundamentals and a passion for digital content, who can translate ideas into engaging visual experiences across multiple digital platforms.

You’ll collaborate with the designers and creative teams to deliver high-quality, on-brand digital assets that support campaigns, product launches, and brand growth. The ideal candidate is creative, detail-oriented, confident, and thrives in a fast-paced, dynamic environment where multiple projects move simultaneously.

Key Responsibilities

  • Create engaging app splash screens, launch screens, and branded in-product visual moments

  • Develop motion concepts, micro-interactions, and lightweight animations to enhance usability and engagement

  • Produce custom illustrations, icons, and visual assets that strengthen the product’s visual identity

  • Design engaging digital assets for apps, websites, social media, email campaigns, paid ads, and landing pages

  • Create visually compelling graphics that align with brand guidelines and design systems

  • Optimise designs for performance across different platforms and devices

  • Prepare and deliver final assets in required formats and specifications

  • Stay up to date with digital design trends, tools, and best practices

Skills & Experience

  • 3–5 years’ experience in a digital design, graphic design, or creative role

  • Strong portfolio showcasing digital-first work (UI, web, social, email)

  • Proficiency in Figma and Adobe Creative Suite

  • Understanding of platform specifications, and responsive design principles

  • Basic knowledge of motion graphics or video editing

  • Strong attention to detail and layout skills

  • Ability to manage multiple projects and meet deadlines

  • Excellent communication and teamwork skills

  • Creative thinker with a strong eye for aesthetics and detail

Preferred Skills

  • Experience with motion design, micro-interactions, or animations.

  • Skilled in digital illustration

  • Interest in AI, emerging technologies, and innovative digital experiences.

What You’ll Get

  • Competitive salary based on experience.

  • Remote-first setup with regular meetups and creative workshops.

  • Opportunities to travel across the UK, USA, and Dubai for collaboration and events.

  • Exposure to cutting-edge AI and product innovation projects.

  • Supportive, forward-thinking culture with mentorship and professional growth opportunities.

  • Be part of a dynamic global group where creativity and technology come together to build the future.

Who You’ll Work With: You'll collaborate directly with designers and creative teams across our global teams, with mentorship from senior members of the product and design team.

To Apply and Progress: Please complete our application form using the link below.

https://hiring.hausofapps.com/digital-designer

apiJob.title: Content Marketing Lead
apiJob.description:
About Envato


At Envato, everything we do is about empowering creatives to thrive.


We're here to help creatives bring their ideas to life, supercharge their workflows, and equip them with an unparalleled toolkit they can use anywhere. With unlimited access to world-class assets, innovative AI tools, and hands-on tutorials, we deliver what creatives need today - and are continually evolving our offering. As AI transforms the creative landscape, we’re committed to building what’s next for the future.


We’re a purpose-driven company and we’ve long embraced flexible, inclusive ways of working. We’re proud of the connected culture we’ve built across our global team. Our values: Community Spirit, Build Our Future, Embrace Diversity, Make It Happen, and Make Envato Proud, guide everything we do, shaping how we work, grow, and succeed.


You can read more about us on our blog, including news of our acquisition by Shutterstock Inc in 2024.


About the Role


As the Content Marketing Lead, you will work with the Senior Content Marketing Manager to execute high-impact content initiatives that inspire and support Envato's global community of creative professionals. This role is central to bringing Envato's positioning to life through compelling content across our key owned channels: the blog, YouTube, and social media.

\n


What you'll be doing
  • Content Production: Execute high-quality content across design, video, and audio that demonstrates hybrid AI-traditional workflows, ensuring brand standards while resonating with creative professionals.
  • Editorial Management: Manage freelance writers, video creators, and experts through briefing, feedback, and coordination while maintaining editorial standards and authentic voices.
  • Multi-Channel Optimisation: Adapt content for blog, YouTube, and social platforms, optimising for SEO/AEO and transforming hero content into multi-format campaigns.
  • Technical Expertise: Maintain deep knowledge of creative tools (Adobe, Figma, DaVinci) and AI developments, serving as internal expert on hybrid workflows and emerging trends.
  • Cross-Functional Collaboration: Partner with product marketing, brand, SEO, CRM and social teams to support launches, integrated campaigns, and seasonal content.
  • Strategic Development: Create educational content bridging traditional and AI-enhanced techniques, showcasing how Envato's tools complement professional software while driving adoption.


About you
  • Deep creative workflow expertise with hands-on proficiency in traditional creative software and emerging AI tools. Able to explain complex technical concepts accessibly.
  • Proven track record producing engaging content for creative professionals, demonstrating understanding of how designers, video editors, and creators work.
  • Content production management including freelancer direction, SEO optimisation, multi-channel adaptation, and ability to scale production while balancing multiple projects.
  • Strategic capabilities including data interpretation for optimisation, prompt engineering, visual judgment, and experience working in cross-functional remote teams at the AI-creativity intersection.


\n

Life at Envato


• Flexible Working: remote-first across Australia or New Zealand with flexibility for you to manage work and life as you need

• Work from Anywhere: apply to work in another location for up to 3 months per calendar year.

• Connection Budget: an annual budget to connect with the team IRL where and when possible.

• Learning Budget: study leave and an annual budget for you to put towards your learning and development.

• Home Office Allowance: a contribution towards your set-up and a monthly allowance to support work from home costs.

• Wellbeing: access to a holistic assistance program that provides support in many areas of life and health.

• Volunteer Leave: 2 days per calendar year to volunteer and give back to the community.

• Parental Leave:20 weeks paid primary carer leave, and 6 weeks paid secondary carers leave.


We appreciate the time you’ve taken to apply to Envato and we're committed to responding to every applicant.


By submitting your application you are agreeing to our Privacy Policy. If you would like to understand how we handle personal information before submitting your application, please read through our Privacy Policy.


Research shows candidates from neurodiverse and underrepresented backgrounds often only apply for roles if they meet all the criteria. We like to make our hiring decisions based on lived experience, so if you’re keen to apply and need reasonable adjustments please let us know.


#LI-Remote

apiJob.description:
Art Digital Art Digital Art Software Apple Design Awards 2024: another win for Procreate News By Joe Foley published 8 June 2024 And Vision Pro apps get a look in with a new spatial computing category. When you purchase through links on our site, we may earn an affiliate commission. Here’s how it works. Signup + Seasonal (around events) Brand Impact Awards Sign up to Creative Bloq's daily newsletter, which brings you the latest news and inspiration from the worlds of art, design and technology. Signup + An account already exists for this email address, please log in. Subscribe to our newsletter The winners are in for the Apple Design Awards 2024, and we're not surprised to see a third accolade for Procreate. This time the team behind the digital art app won the app innovation award for Procreate Dreams, a powerful animation app for iPad (see our own five-star Procreate Dreams review. Elsewhere in this year's awards, there was a new category, spatial computing, following the launch of Apple's Vision Pro headset. In total, 14 apps and games won awards for design, technical achievement and innovation. Procreate Dreams is a 2D animation app for iPad (Image credit: Procreate)The winners of the Apple Design Awards 2024 were chosen from 42 finalists. One app and one game were chosen in each of seven categories: delight and fun, inclusivity, innovation, interaction, social impact, visuals and graphics and the new spatial computing category. You may like The best drawing apps for iPad Procreate Dreams 2 makes the iPad animation app even more powerful and versatile From Wicked to KPop Demon Hunters, see the Concept Art Awards 2025 winners in full Having already its second accolade in the Apple Design Awards 2022, Procreate picked up the innovation award for apps with Procreate Dreams. Apple praised the 2D animation app for including the extensive library of brushes, gestures, and PencilKit-enabled behaviors from the original Procreate and for its support for multitouch interactions and Apple Pencil.Winners in the visuals and graphics category were Rooms from Things Inc, an app and social space for building imaginative scenes, and Lies of P from NEOWIZ, an adventure game that allows players to control a robotic puppet. Here Apple commended the textures, detailed lighting and effects and visual customization options like MetalFX upscaling and volumetric fog effects on Mac. Algoriddim's Djay Pro won the inaugural edition of the spatial computing category (Image credit: Algoriddim)The winners in the new spatial computing category were Algoriddim's Djay Pro - DJ App & AI Mixer, a virtual DJ app complete with turntables, interactive effects and a range of virtual environments, and Shapes and Stories' Blackbox a puzzle game that encourages users to explore the spatial environment. apiJob.title: Design
apiJob.description: DesignWe're a cross-disciplinary community that loves to create great experiences that make meaningful connections between listeners and creators.Design at Spotify isn’t one discipline working in isolation. Far from it. Instead, we work in unison with every other team, infusing our user-centered and technology-empowered strategy across all areas of the business.We value being close to the people we serve, so we embed our experts across business units and work with them as one. We still share design standards and systems across our community, things like common tools, design, and language. But to create a truly cohesive experience for our users across products and platforms, we’re all about collaboration.Product DesignProduct Designers focus on delivering the best experience for listeners, creators, and external partners across all of our products and markets. We bring fulfillment and value to listeners and creators and make it easy for people to enjoy Spotify every day, exactly how they want.Editorial DesignEditorial Design focuses on art direction and collaborates with our product, brand, marketing, and content practices. We build audience-specific, culture-defining brands, and product experiences for Spotify listeners worldwide.Internal Tools DesignOur Internal Tools experts create the technology that enables Spotify to learn quickly and scale easily. We build the frameworks, capabilities, and platforms needed to welcome the world’s listeners, supporting rapid growth in our users and business.Design OpsDesign Ops supports our practice and community by turning down design pain and dialing up design joy. We infuse best-in-class program management with design thinking to improve the pillars of our practice: tools and systems; learning and inspiration; and culture and community.UX WritingHere at Spotify, UX Writing is part of Design. We specialize in intuitive user-centered content strategy and microcopy, but our work doesn’t end there. We partner with design, tech, and product to create cohesive experiences with a consistent voice, ensuring we always sound like Spotify.Still curious?Read the stories behind some of our best work. Get to know our Design community a little better. Learn how we’re leading the industry and building meaningful experiences and connections through everything we do. Just head to our dedicated website, Spotify Design.Go to the blogHey, wanna join the band?Fresh opportunitiesLocation Job type Hang on, just getting the latest stats...Fresh opportunitiesFilters (1)Design0 jobs inall locations inall job types NerdOut@Spotify, 33: Ship Happens (When Agents Code)ListenNerdOut@Spotify, 32: ARM-ing Ourselves with Google Axion, Part 2ListenNerdOut@Spotify, 31: ARM-ing Ourselves with Google Axion, Part 1ListenNerdOut@Spotify, 30: Building AiKA: Spotify’s AI Knowledge AssistantListenNerdOut@Spotify, 29: Deploying Our New Typeface: Spotify MixListenWho we are01One band, no solo artistsIt’s our culture. It’s our values. It’s who we are and what we’re not. It’s why we do things the way we do and why that matters. It’s all here in our band manifesto.Read moreOur culture02Nine to five? No thanksClock on, clock off just isn’t our vibe. We want you to feel like you can come to work with a spring in your step, passion for what you do, and the desire to keep growing.Read moreEquity & impact03Everyone has a part to playWe don’t just want you to feel like you belong here, we want you to feel like you can thrive here. No two creators or listeners are the same, so at Spotify, neither are we.Read moreQuick clicksExplore our global locationsLocationsStudents, make an impact not the coffeesStudentsRead about Equity, Diversity & ImpactEquity, Diversity & Impact

apiJob.title: Motion Designer
apiJob.description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancyMotion DesignerDepartment: MarketingLocations: London, New YorkJob Requisition ID: JR001588Apply nowAbout the Team The Brand team at Miro is responsible for how the company shows up in the world: from campaigns and product launches to events and social channels. We're a small, collaborative group of creatives who work across the full spectrum of brand expression, partnering closely with product marketing, corporate marketing, and events. We move fast, care deeply about craft, and are increasingly leaning into AI tools to push what's possible. About the Role We're looking for an ambitious, well-rounded Motion Designer to join our Brand team. This person understands that motion design is storytelling - you're not just animating, you're communicating ideas and driving action. You have a point of view on what makes advertising work and how motion can elevate a brand. The right person has great taste, strong 2D animation fundamentals, and can work across the full production process: from storyboarding through editing and sound. You're comfortable working fast and producing at volume. And you're curious about AI content generation tools - not necessarily an expert, but eager to experiment and learn. We're building a campaign right now that's produced entirely with AI, and we need someone who wants to be part of that. What you’ll do Create motion content for product launches, brand campaigns, social, and events like Canvas • Own projects end-to-end: concept, storyboard, animation, editing, and sound design • Translate complex product features into clear, engaging visual stories • Collaborate with product marketing, corporate marketing, and events teams • Experiment with AI image and video tools to create content at scale • Help evolve Miro's motion design language and maintain consistency across touchpoints What you’ll need 5+ years of professional motion design experience Exceptional 2D animation skills with a portfolio that demonstrates strong taste and timing Experience across the full production cycle - storyboarding, animation, editing, sound Comfort working fast and managing multiple projects with competing deadlines Familiarity with AI image/video generation tools (Runway especially) and eagerness to learn more Self-direction: you take a brief and run with it without heavy oversight What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. Join a diverse team where you can do your best work. Full benefits may differ per location. If you would like to learn more about location-specific benefits, please refer to our Global Miro benefits board. #LI-DNIThe reasonably estimated salary range is specific to New York and may not be applicable to other locations. Final compensation and total package components will be based on individual factors such as the candidate's skills, qualifications, and experience. The range for this role is:New York salary range$116,000—$174,000 USDAbout Miro Miro is a visual workspace for innovation that enables distributed teams of any size to build the next big thing. The platform's infinite canvas enables teams to lead engaging workshops and meetings, design products, brainstorm ideas, and more. Miro, co-headquartered in San Francisco and Amsterdam, serves more than 100M users and 250,000 companies collaborate in the Innovation Workspace. Miro was founded in 2011 and currently has more than 1,600 employees in 13 hubs around the world. We are a team of dreamers. We look for individuals who dream big, work hard, and above all stay humble. Collaboration is at the heart of what we do and through our work together we hope to create a supportive, welcoming, and innovative environment. We strive to play as a team to win the world and create a better version of ourselves every day. If this sounds like something that excites you, we want to hear from you! Check out more about life at Miro: Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@lifeatmiro Blog: https://miro.com/careers/life-at-miro/all/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mirohq/ At Miro, we strive to create and foster an environment of belonging and collaboration across cultural differences. Miro’s mission — Empower teams to create the next big thing — is how we think about our product, people, and culture. We believe that creating big things requires diverse and inclusive teams. Diversity invites all talent with different demography, identities and styles to step in, and inclusion invites them to step closer together. Every day, we are working to build a more diverse Miro, cultivate a sense of belonging for future and current Mironeers around the world, and foster an environment where everyone can collaborate and embrace differences. apiJob.title: Visual Communications Designer
apiJob.description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancyVisual Communications DesignerDepartment: MarketingLocations: Amsterdam, LondonJob Requisition ID: JR001715Apply nowAbout the Team The Miro Brand Creative team is 15 people—designers, producers, project managers, creatives and AI specialists. We're spread across Europe but work with the kind of consistency and closeness you'd expect from a team sat in the same room. High standards, fast pace, and a genuinely varied output that's hard to find at this level elsewhere. About the Role Miro needs a dedicated, senior-level Visual Communications Designer. The immediate priority is Canvas 26—our flagship annual event across four cities—where you'll own the keynote deck end-to-end. But this is a full-time role with a much wider remit: executive and CMO communications, board decks, all-hands, investor materials, and sales enablement. You'll work closely with senior leadership including the CMO, embedded directly in the Miro moments that count. What you’ll do Own the Canvas 26 keynote end-to-end across four cities — San Francisco, London, Tokyo, and Sydney — adapting and iterating as speakers and content evolve Design and deliver all executive and CMO presentations, board decks, all-hands, and sales enablement materials Work directly with senior leadership to shape and refine presentations in real time Transform complex product narratives, strategy, and data into clear, compelling visual stories Build and maintain a presentation system — templates, assets, and standards — that brings consistency across the business Push the craft — typography, layout, data visualisation, animation — and set a new bar for what presentation design looks like at Miro Your work will be seen by thousands of people across four global cities — this is as high visibility as presentation design gets What you’ll need 5+ years of presentation design experience working with C-suite or senior leadership stakeholders A portfolio demonstrating range across keynotes, executive decks, data visualisation, and event content Expert-level Keynote skills including animations, transitions, and build orders Strong working knowledge of Figma, Illustrator, and Photoshop The ability to work fast without losing craft quality, managing multiple priorities simultaneously A strong creative POV and the confidence to defend it—and the good sense to know when to let it go Comfortable with ambiguity, last-minute changes, and the pace that comes with working directly alongside senior leadership What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. Join a diverse team where you can do your best work. Full benefits may differ per location. If you would like to learn more about location-specific benefits, please refer to our Global Miro benefits board. #LI-DNIAbout Miro Miro is a visual workspace for innovation that enables distributed teams of any size to build the next big thing. The platform's infinite canvas enables teams to lead engaging workshops and meetings, design products, brainstorm ideas, and more. Miro, co-headquartered in San Francisco and Amsterdam, serves more than 100M users and 250,000 companies collaborate in the Innovation Workspace. Miro was founded in 2011 and currently has more than 1,600 employees in 13 hubs around the world. We are a team of dreamers. We look for individuals who dream big, work hard, and above all stay humble. Collaboration is at the heart of what we do and through our work together we hope to create a supportive, welcoming, and innovative environment. We strive to play as a team to win the world and create a better version of ourselves every day. If this sounds like something that excites you, we want to hear from you! Check out more about life at Miro: Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@lifeatmiro Blog: https://miro.com/careers/life-at-miro/all/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mirohq/ At Miro, we strive to create and foster an environment of belonging and collaboration across cultural differences. Miro’s mission — Empower teams to create the next big thing — is how we think about our product, people, and culture. We believe that creating big things requires diverse and inclusive teams. Diversity invites all talent with different demography, identities and styles to step in, and inclusion invites them to step closer together. Every day, we are working to build a more diverse Miro, cultivate a sense of belonging for future and current Mironeers around the world, and foster an environment where everyone can collaborate and embrace differences. apiJob.title: Senior Product Designer - License Management
apiJob.description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancySenior Product Designer - License ManagementDepartment: DesignLocations: LondonJob Requisition ID: JR000758Apply nowAbout the Team Miro’s Design team is on a mission to improve the experience of knowledge work, productivity tools, and remote collaboration. We produce best-in-class solutions for a future of work that’s diverse, intuitive, accessible, and enjoyable to use. Maybe that’s why our product is so globally beloved. The Growth team is dedicated to driving new customer acquisition and expanding our user base. A highly-analytical and data-informed team, we work closely with cross-functional partners to design and implement strategies that fuel Miro's growth. Our focus is on creating data-driven, user-centric designs that balance business goals with exceptional user experiences. About the Role The Senior Product Designer for the License Management team will design foundational systems at the intersection of user experience, scale, and monetisation. You will tackle complex, high-leverage challenges that shape how millions of customers access and pay for Miro, as well as designing and shipping experiments that drive FR and Guest users towards paid seats, and activate paid seats to drive MAUs. What you’ll do Lead the design of growth-focused features and experiments from ideation through launch, iterating based on insights from user research and quantitative data Collaborate with PMs, Engineers, Data Analysts, and other designers to define hypotheses, design experiments, and measure impact on growth metrics Translate data insights and user feedback into thoughtful, intuitive design solutions that help drive customer growth, product usage, and revenue Apply your understanding of SaaS growth concepts, including customer acquisition, activation, retention, and monetization to your design work for product-led growth and enterprise users Balance business objectives with user needs, ensuring both are reflected in your process and deliverables Advocate for high design quality and consistency, collaborating with the design system team to ensure solutions align with our broader product experience Communicate design rationale and solutions clearly to stakeholders, proactively seeking feedback and alignment Learn from and collaborate with a talented team of designers, sharing feedback and growing your craftStay current on growth design best practices, experimentation methodologies, and industry trends to inform and inspire your work Explore using AI tools to speed up validation, design discovery, and prototyping What you’ll need 5+ years of product design experience, ideally in B2B SaaS or growth-focused roles, with a portfolio showcasing your design thinking and impact Experience designing and running data-driven experiments, and iterating based on results Experience working on projects that have a direct impact on product usage or revenue Good understanding of core growth concepts for SaaS products and platforms (e.g., user acquisition, onboarding, retention). Ability to balance business goals with user needs, delivering high-quality, user-centric designs Strong collaboration skills with cross-functional partners, including PMs, Engineers, Data Analysts, and other designers Proficiency in Figma and prototyping tools; experience with analytics platforms is a plus Clear communication and openness to feedback, with a desire to learn and grow as part of a team Growth mindset and a passion for experimentation Experience using AI tools to accelerate validation, prototyping, or design discovery Experience with enterprise applications and services is a plus What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. apiJob.title: Senior Content Designer
apiJob.description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancySenior Content DesignerDepartment: DesignLocations: LondonJob Requisition ID: JR001613Apply nowAbout the Team We have a small but mighty content design team at Miro, with just three people across multiple key initiatives. This means we must be flexible, resilient and good at prioritising projects where we can have the biggest impact! We're an integral part of the Design org, shaping the user experience for some of our most impactful initiatives. We operate within a hybrid model, embedding deeply within high-priority cross-functional teams to craft user-centered content that guides and delights our users, and offering light support to lower priority work. About the Role We’re looking for a talented Content Designer and AI whizz to join our team and contribute directly to high-impact projects that elevate the Miro experience. This role will sit within our AI Canvas team, specifically working on enhancing our AI Workflow experiences. You'll partner with senior leadership across product, design, and engineering, and build the frameworks that help other teams create consistent AI content at scale. You’ll also be a key player in shaping the content design discipline at Miro, collaborating closely with product managers, designers, researchers, and product marketers and delivering content that enhances usability and drives adoption. This is an exciting opportunity to make a meaningful impact on our product and be part of growing the maturity of our content design practice. What you’ll do Work with product, UX, and engineering teams to help shape the product vision and create user-focused content for high-priority AI features and initiatives – ensuring clarity and consistency across the product Design and develop prompts, UI copy and error messages to help our users adopt Sidekicks and Flows and get high quality results when using them Plan, develop and deliver conversational content that guides users effectively, reduces cognitive load, and aligns with Miro’s voice and tone guidelines Contribute to the refinement and application of Miro’s content design guidelines, particularly focused on refining our AI principles and guidance which will ultimately need to live in our design system and MCPs Collaborate with design and UX research to conduct user research, test content hypotheses, and iterate solutions based on data and feedback Participate in design and product reviews, providing clear rationale for your content design decisions Advocate for the value of content design, helping to integrate the discipline into the broader product development process On a typical day you'll be: Writing and testing system prompts, then debugging the model Defining evaluation criteria with engineering and reviewing AI outputs against quality benchmarks Shaping conversation flows and interaction patterns in design crits and tiger teams Running through edge cases to figure out where the AI breaks and how to recover gracefully Creating conversation starters and prompt suggestions that teach users what's actually possible What you’ll need 6+ years of experience in content design or UX writing, with a strong background in creating content for digital products A strong portfolio showcasing your ability to create clear, user-focused content and demonstrating the impact on user experience and business goals Proven experience working closely with cross-functional teams, including product management, UX design, and engineering, to deliver seamless content solutions Deep understanding of UX writing principles, information architecture, and user-centered design methodologies Familiarity with AI products, conversation design, or prompt engineering (you don't need to be an engineer, but you should be comfortable getting hands-on with how AI systems behave) Ability to align product content strategy with business objectives, balancing user needs with technical feasibility Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to articulate the value of content design and collaborate effectively across diverse teams A proactive and flexible approach, comfortable working autonomously in a fast-paced, sometimes ambiguous environment and managing multiple priorities What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. Join a diverse team where you can do your best work. Full benefits may differ per location. apiJob.title: Graduate Product Designer
apiJob.description: Our StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsMenuOur StoryPeopleHow we hireTeamsEngineering teamDesign teamProduct teamCustomer experience teamLocationsLife at MiroOpen positionsHomeOpen positionsVacancyGraduate Product DesignerDepartment: DesignLocations: Amsterdam, BerlinJob Requisition ID: JR001668Apply nowAbout the Team At Miro, the design team is fully embedded in various product streams while operating as a tight-knit, centralized team of designers, researchers & content writers. While our processes are fully consolidated into the product development life cycle, we still enjoy a high degree of ownership and autonomy in our work. We’re striving to find the right balance between visual craftsmanship, user needs, and business goals. You’ll apply your experience and education with a customer-centric mindset to solve customer problems and identify business opportunities. We operate in a fast-paced environment and get to have a direct impact on the millions of users who rely on Miro to collaborate and create the next big thing. As a part of our Product Design team, you will contribute to our design process, culture, and experience principles and set new standards for design quality. Learn more about the colorful design team and our culture: https://miro.design/You'll apply a customer-centric approach to complex problem-solving and collaborate with other designers from some of the highest-profile companies in the world. We operate in a fast-paced environment and get to have a direct impact on the millions of users who rely on Miro to collaborate and create the next big thing. As a part of our Product Design team, you will contribute to our design process, culture, and experience principles and set new standards for design quality. Learn more about the colorful design team and our culture: https://miro.design/ About the Role The Miro Graduate Product Design Programme is designed to help new designers launch their careers in design, research or content writing. In our 12-month programme, you'll gain hands-on experience working on real-world projects. You'll also be mentored by experienced designers, researchers, and content writers, who will help you develop your skills and career. As a graduate product designer at Miro, you will focus on learning, growing and bringing a new fresh perspective to the Miro experience. Your goal will be to explore the customer experience, identify opportunities, explore the fields of product design, UXR, and content design, and deliver high-quality solutions but most of all to learn and grow as a new designer at Miro and find your place from Product Design, Research, Content Design or maybe somewhere else. What you’ll do Explore the end-to-end design process, from discovery research to effective problem framing, ideation, co-creation, prototyping, and user testing, into high-fidelity designs Identify where you need to dive deep, and running your own research and collaborate closely with UXR and analytics to turn insights into high-quality outcomes Work not just on designs, but the story we tell and work closely with content design to elevate the product beyond visuals. Understand the success of your solutions with the team and iterate on them closely with engineering partners to ensure solid implementation in production. Understand the competitive landscape, and using qualitative and quantitative methods hypothesize new solutions and improvements to the Miro products. Bring delight to Miro users through customer centric solutions implemented thought high-quality interaction design and creative visuals.. What you’ll need Graduate in 2025-6 in Bachelor’s or Master’s in interaction design, industrial design, creative design, HCI, or a related design field Strong drive to learn, and absorb knowledge from your team, and ability to take and utilize this knowledge in your work AI-First Proficiency - We’re building for an AI-first future, and we’re looking for graduates who aren’t just using AI—they’re mastering it. Effective written and verbal communication skills Be available to start a full time position for our 12 month programme from August 2026 Be able to commute to our office in Amsterdam or Berlin What's in it for you We want you to feel supported, connected, and ready to grow. Our global benefits package generally includes equity, a wellbeing benefit, a WFH equipment allowance, and an annual Learning & Development stipend. Join a diverse team where you can do your best work. Full benefits may differ per location. If you would like to learn more about location-specific benefits, please refer to our Global Miro benefits board.A note on location:We’d love to hear from you! Please keep in mind that we are currently unable to provide relocation assistance for this role. If you are applying from outside the Netherlands or Berlin, you would need to be prepared to manage your own relocation and any associated costs.About Miro Miro is a visual workspace for innovation that enables distributed teams of any size to build the next big thing. The platform's infinite canvas enables teams to lead engaging workshops and meetings, design products, brainstorm ideas, and more. Miro, co-headquartered in San Francisco and Amsterdam, serves more than 100M users and 250,000 companies collaborate in the Innovation Workspace. Miro was founded in 2011 and currently has more than 1,600 employees in 13 hubs around the world. We are a team of dreamers. We look for individuals who dream big, work hard, and above all stay humble. Collaboration is at the heart of what we do and through our work together we hope to create a supportive, welcoming, and innovative environment. We strive to play as a team to win the world and create a better version of ourselves every day. If this sounds like something that excites you, we want to hear from you! Check out more about life at Miro: Youtube: https://www.youtube.com/@lifeatmiro Blog: https://miro.com/careers/life-at-miro/all/ Instagram: https://www.instagram.com/mirohq/ At Miro, we strive to create and foster an environment of belonging and collaboration across cultural differences. Miro’s mission — Empower teams to create the next big thing — is how we think about our product, people, and culture. We believe that creating big things requires diverse and inclusive teams. Diversity invites all talent with different demography, identities and styles to step in, and inclusion invites them to step closer together. Every day, we are working to build a more diverse Miro, cultivate a sense of belonging for future and current Mironeers around the world, and foster an environment where everyone can collaborate and embrace differences. Miro handles and uses personal data of job applicants in line with its Recruitment Privacy Policy found here. apiJob.title: 3D Animation Artist - Unpublished R&D Product (Contract)
apiJob.description:
Art3D Animation Artist - Unpublished R&D Product (Contract)Guangzhou, ChinaJob Id: 6010职位概述 作为 3D 动作设计师（Contractor），你将加入我们的美术团队，负责为游戏角色、生物或道具制作高质量的关键帧动画（Keyframe Animation）。我们需要一位执行力强、基本功扎实的动画师。你不需要负责复杂的动捕数据清洗或底层的动画系统搭建，但你需要能独立承担一个角色或模块的动画量产工作，确保动画在引擎中表现流畅，并符合项目的艺术风格与玩法需求。 你将与玩法策划、技术美术紧密合作，通过动画赋予角色生命力，为玩家提供极致的打击感与操作反馈。 核心职责 高质量动画制作： 根据需求文档和分镜，使用 Maya 制作高质量的 3D 角色动画（包括但不限于：待机、移动、战斗技能、受击、死亡等）。 风格化表现： 深刻理解并还原项目的艺术风格（例如：美式卡通、强打击感、夸张变形等），确保动作设计符合角色性格（Acting）。 引擎集成与调试： 将动画资源导入游戏引擎（如 Unreal/Unity 或自研引擎），配置动画混合树（Blend Trees）、状态机（State Machines）基础设置，并能够在引擎内预览和验收效果。 玩法手感打磨： 与玩法策划紧密配合，通过调整动画的节奏（Timing/Spacing）和帧数，优化游戏的操作手感和响应速度（Gameplay Feel）。 Bug修复与优化： 负责维护和修复现有动画资源中的穿插、滑步、抖动等问题，确保交付资源的稳定性。 任职要求 经验要求： 3年以上游戏行业 3D 动画制作经验，至少参与过一款完整的游戏项目开发。 专业技能： 精通 Autodesk Maya 的动画模块。 深刻理解动画 12项基本法则（如挤压拉伸、预备动作、跟随动作等），尤其是在人体力学（Body Mechanics）和重量感表现上有扎实功底。 擅长 纯手K（Keyframe） 动画制作，能够处理风格化（Stylized）的角色动态。 沟通与协作： 具备良好的沟通能力（P2核心能力），能够准确理解反馈意见并快速迭代。 能够主动与上下游（策划/模型/特效）对齐需求，预判潜在的制作风险。 作品集： 必须提供 Demo Reel，展示涵盖肢体运动、战斗动作和表演片段的高质量作品。 加分项 引擎经验： 熟悉 Unreal Engine 或 Unity 的动画蓝图/系统。 技术理解： 了解基础的绑定（Rigging）知识，能进行简单的蒙皮（Skinning）修复或给 TA 提供有效的绑定反馈。 多风格适应： 既能驾驭写实风格，也能处理夸张卡通风格。 第一人称经验： 有 FPS（第一人称射击）武器与手部动画制作经验者优先。 用工性质：与专业人事服务公司FESCO Adecco签订劳动合同.Apply

apiJob.title: Content Producer - LoL Esports
apiJob.description:
Production GroupContent Producer - LoL EsportsSeoul, KoreaJob Id: REQ-0009768Riot Games was established in 2006 by avid gamers who believe that player-focused game development can result in great games. In 2009, Riot released its debut title League of Legends to critical and player acclaim. Players form the foundation of our community and it’s for them that we continue to evolve and improve the League of Legends experience, and beyond! We celebrated the 15th year of LoL esports at the 2025 World Championship and we hope to share many more great experiences to our players through esports. We’re looking for passionate professionals who share the same goal to make LoL esports even greater. That's where you come in. As a Content Producer - LoL Esports, you will report into Content Production Lead. You’ll be our Esports video visionary, creating and shepherding technically excellent, fun-heightened, player-approved, gameplay video content from conception to delivery, improving the quality bar for the Content Services Group in Korea. As a Rioter in Seoul, you’ll join a tight-knit group of exceptionally bright-minded individuals who share our prioritization of player experience, rejection of convention, and passion for gaming. Riot Games Korea is dedicated to creating tailored player experiences that show local players they aren’t lost in translation. Responsibilities: Coordinate with a variety of Esports technical, creative, and live broadcast teams, as well as other partners, to guide Esports gameplay video projects through post-production and deliver best-in-class industry results. Infuse creative and planning meetings with fresh ideas for new, electrifying Esports content that’ll blow players away. Take ownership of the defined creative vision and shape content from pre-production to delivery. Evaluate our Esports content, presenting new ways to improve our technical and editorial skills . Ability to manage stakeholder reviews, maintaining detailed communications throughout a video’s process from concept to delivery. Required Qualifications: Experienced: You have 5+ years of work experience as a producer in gaming, sports, or entertainment content production, with a proven ability to create and execute project plans and schedules. You bring strong knowledge of diverse project types (web, mobile, video, brand, strategic consulting) and the capability to develop and produce platform-specific content in a rapidly evolving content ecosystem. A creative story teller: you have the skill and the passion to craft compelling, authentic, and fresh narratives for Esports audiences. You possess production & project management experience with a reel or links demonstrating strong creative video content. A team player: you're open to performing any task, whether big or small; you're willing to teach and learn, lead, and follow. A culture-savvy enthusiast: Whether it’s NFL, NBA, MLB, or EPL, you have a broad interest and passion not only in sports but also entertainment, and cultural trends across the broader arts and culture landscape.You have a strong understanding of why audiences connect with certain cultural phenomena and apply those insights to content planning and storytelling. For this role, you'll find success through craft expertise, a collaborative spirit, and decision-making that prioritizes the delight of players. We will be looking at your past studies, experience, and your personal relationship with games. If you embody player empathy and care about players' experiences, this could be your role!Apply

apiJob.description:
PublishingContent Producer - Riftbound (Contract)Shanghai, ChinaJob Id: 6019Riot Games was established in 2006 by entrepreneurial gamers who believe that player-focused game development can result in great games. In 2009, Riot released its debut title ---- League of Legends to critical and player acclaim. As the most played video game in the world, over 100 million play every month. Players form the foundation of our community and it’s for them that we continue to evolve and improve the experience.​ We’re looking for humble but ambitious, razor-sharp professionals who can teach us a thing or two. We promise to return the favor. Like us, you take play seriously; you’re passionate about games. We embrace those who see things differently, aren’t afraid to experiment, and who have a healthy disregard for constraints.​ That's where you come in.​ As Content Producer – Publishing, Riftbound, you will report to the Regional Publishing Lead. Your role will involve translating our strategic goals into creative campaigns that surprise and delight our players. Partnering internally and externally across various disciplines, you will ensure a smooth creative production process and assets management process. Your awareness and transparent communication will provide you with a comprehensive understanding of our campaign requirements, allowing you to advocate for their needs and ensure they are fulfilled.​ Responsibilities​ Assist in developing and executing content strategies aligned with team goals Work with publishers or vendors to ensure timely production and delivery of high-quality content; coordinate with publishers or vendors to ensure timely production and delivery of high-quality content Monitor and analyze content performance metrics to support optimization of future content Collaborate closely with the team to ensure content supports overall business objectives.​ Maintain a content calendar to ensure consistent publication schedules Participate in content audits to ensure quality and consistency Provide day-to-day feedback on the visual design production process and ensure accuracy in deliveries.​ Knowledge & Skills​ Expertise in project and production management, with proven experience in content management and strategy, encompassing all facets of campaign, video, and art production.​ Strong attention to detail with excellent writing, editing, and communication skills to evaluate and ensure high standards in content output.​ Proficient in managing a high volume of creative and art marketing assets, adept at maintaining consistency and quality across multiple projects Understanding of the visual design production process.​ Highly proficient communication skills and capacity.​ Required Qualifications​ At least 3 years of experience in the relevant fields, with a proven track record in delivering marketing campaigns and managing art assets Fluent in spoken English and Mandarin.​ Basic understanding of advertising, production, and/or project management Passionate or experience in game marketing.​ Desired Qualifications​ Interest or experience in game marketing, card games or TCG preferably Proficiency in Adobe Creative Suite or similar tools for delivery validation.​ Expertise in project management tools and digital asset management systems.​ Card game collector/TCG player or fans with strong familiarity of the IP/ community. Employment Type: employment contract with FESCO Adecco, a professional personnel company in China.Apply

apiJob.title: Game Designer III, Competitive Systems- VALORANT
apiJob.description:
DesignGame Designer III, Competitive Systems- VALORANTLos Angeles, USAAdditional Locations: Mercer Island, USAJob Id: REQ-0008434As a Senior Game Designer, you will design, tweak, and optimize the player experience. Your analytical skills, player insight, and creativity aid in crafting in- and out-of-game experiences that engage and surprise players. You will collaborate with interdisciplinary teams and work with every tool at hand to innovate and iterate player-focused designs that'll delight millions around the world. VALORANT is Riot’s take on a character-based tactical shooter (for more info on VALORANT click here!). As a Game Designer III on the Competitive Systems team, you’ll design and deliver world-class competitive and ranked systems that celebrate mastery, fairness, and player growth. You’ll develop a deep understanding of what drives competitive players and design systems that are both strategically sound and deeply satisfying. You’ll also craft solutions that address diverse player experiences across platforms, ensuring competitive integrity and engagement for all. This role reports to the Lead Metasystems Designer. Responsibilities: Design and evolve competitive and ranked systems that reward player skill, growth, and long-term investment in VALORANT. Develop and support matchmaking systems that ensure fair and balanced competition. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to bring new features from concept to live deployment. Identify and explore new design opportunities in partnership with Product Managers and Design Leadership. Create multiple design solutions with clear articulation of trade-offs, risks, and alignment with product and player goals. Playtest VALORANT regularly, providing feedback to improve existing systems and prototype new ideas. Required Qualifications: 5+ years of professional Game Design experience. Shipped or contributed to at least one AAA+ online competitive title. Demonstrated ability to design and iterate on complex systems with a clear understanding of player impact and downstream effects. Strong communication skills with proven ability to articulate design rationale and decision frameworks to team members. Desired Qualifications: Deep passion and understanding of competitive online games and their player communities. Experience developing matchmaking, ranked, or progression systems. Familiarity with online shooters, especially VALORANT or tactical shooters. Ability to interpret and learn from charged or emotional player feedback to improve the product experience. Experience balancing competitive integrity with engaging and rewarding gameplay systems. Experience developing across multiple platforms (PC, Mobile, Console). For this role, you'll find success through craft expertise and a collaborative spirit that prioritizes the delight of players. We will look at your past studies and experience, but for this role, we also look for dedicated people with a personal relationship with games. If you embody player empathy and care about players' experiences, this is the role for you! Our Perks: Riot has a focus on work/life balance, shown by our open paid time off policy, in addition to other perks such as flexible work schedules. We offer medical, dental, and life insurance, parental leave for you, your spouse/domestic partner and children, and a 401k with company match. Check out our benefits pages for more information. At Riot Games, we put players first. That mission drives every decision in our quest to create games and experiences that make it better to be a player. Whether you’re working directly on a new player-facing experience or you’re supporting the company as a whole, everyone at Riot is part of our mission. And just like in our games, we’re better when we work together. Our goal is to create collaborative teams where you are empowered to bring your unique perspective everyday. If that sounds like the kind of place you want to work, we’re looking forward to your application. It’s our policy to provide equal employment opportunity for all applicants and members of Riot Games, Inc. Riot Games makes reasonable accommodations for handicapped and disabled Rioters and does not unlawfully discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, national origin, age, handicap, veteran status, marital status, criminal history, or any other category protected by applicable federal and state law. We consider for employment all qualified applicants, including those with criminal histories, in a manner consistent with applicable federal, state and local law, including the California Fair Chance Act, the City of Los Angeles Fair Chance Initiative for Hiring Ordinance, the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance for Employers, the San Francisco Fair Chance Ordinance, and the Washington Fair Chance Act. Per the Los Angeles County Fair Chance Ordinance, the following core duties may create a basis for disqualifying candidates with relevant criminal histories: Safeguarding confidential and sensitive Company data Communication with others, including Rioters and third parties such as vendors, and/or players, including minors Accessing Company assets, secure digital systems, and networks Ensuring a safe interactive environment for players and other Rioters These duties are directly related to essential operations, safety, trust, and compliance obligations within our organization. Please note that job duties may evolve based on business needs and additional responsibilities may be assigned as necessary to maintain operational efficiency and security. (Los Angeles Only) Base salary range between $150,000.00 - $209,600.00 USD + incentive compensation + equity + 401K with company match + medical, dental, vision, and life insurance + short and long-term disability + open PTO.(Mercer Island only) Base salary range between $150,000.00 - $209,600.00 USD + incentive compensation + equity + 401K with company match + medical, dental, vision, and life insurance + short and long-term disability + open PTO.Apply

apiJob.title: Game Designer, Combat - Unpublished R&D Product (Contract)
apiJob.description:
DesignGame Designer, Combat - Unpublished R&D Product (Contract)Shanghai, ChinaJob Id: 5920作为一名游戏设计师，你将负责设计、调整并优化玩家体验。你出色的分析能力、对玩家的洞察力以及创造力将共同打造游戏内外令人惊喜且具有吸引力的玩家体验。你将与跨学科团队合作，充分运用各种工具，持续迭代和优化以玩家为核心的设计，致力于让全球数百万玩家感到满意与喜爱。 在研发（R&D）团队中担任战斗游戏设计师，负责一个早期阶段的产品，你将参与揭示 Riot 下一款重点项目的方向。你将与其他设计师、艺术家和工程师密切协作，优化和迭代以玩家为中心的设计，致力于创造突破性的游戏体验。由于团队规模仍较小，该岗位需要大量实操工作，亲自创建和调校战斗系统/机制。你应能适应目标与限制主要由团队驱动的不确定性环境，同时适应大大小小的变更作为设计流程的日常一部分。你将向项目的战斗主管汇报工作。 职责： 设计、实现并改进战斗机制与系统，使其令玩家满意并提升对游戏概念的信心。 在所处游戏类型中探索创新战斗设计方案，提出多种可行的设计与解决方案，清晰说明各种方案之间的权衡与风险。 利用快速原型与游戏测试验证设计方案的可行性。 编写清晰的设计文档，确保非设计人员也能评估与实现相关内容。 与设计团队及其他跨职能团队协作，共同打造出色的整体游戏体验。积极配合、听取反馈并不断迭代，以实现最高质量的产品。 岗位要求： 至少 3 年专业游戏设计工作经验。 至少 2 年 Unreal 引擎使用经验，熟练使用技能编辑器、关卡编辑器、GAS 系统及蓝图。 在以下领域至少精通一项：战斗机制、战斗手感、战斗 AI、角色控制 / 摄像机 / 操作系统。 对 PvE 动作游戏 / ARPG 类型有深入理解与热情，并积极参与相关游戏体验。 能够流利使用中文普通话交流。 加分项： 在至少一款已发行游戏中，熟练使用某款游戏引擎的脚本编程。 熟悉《英雄联盟》宇宙与 IP。 能流利使用英语进行口头与书面交流。 在此岗位中，你将通过精湛的专业技能、积极的协作精神和以玩家愉悦体验为导向的决策获得成功。我们将重点参考你过往的学习经历、工作经验及你与游戏的个人关系。如果你具有强烈的玩家同理心，并真正关心玩家的游戏体验，这份工作可能正是为你量身打造的！ 用工性质：与专业人事服务公司FESCO Adecco签订劳动合同Apply

apiJob.title: Level Design Lead - VALORANT
apiJob.description:
DesignLevel Design Lead - VALORANTLos Angeles, USAAdditional Locations: Mercer Island, USAJob Id: REQ-0009665As a Game Design Manager / Level Design Lead you'll be front and center in defining the design of our game. You'll partner with leads from multiple disciplines to ensure that VALORANT’s tactical gameplay shines through. You'll be a champion for design excellence, and level up those around you. You’ll apply rigorous critical thinking and a strong dose of imagination to engineer the fun of thrilling in-game experiences. You'll work with every tool at hand and collaborate with multiple interdisciplinary teams to create player-focused designs that'll delight millions around the world. VALORANT is Riot’s take on a character-driven tactical shooter—one where every angle matters, every decision carries weight, and competitive integrity is sacred. As Level Design Lead, you’ll help define the future of VALORANT’s maps: evolving existing spaces, leading bold new innovations, and ensuring every map delivers on the tactical depth players expect. You’ll partner closely with design, art, and production leadership to shape how millions of players experience the game—today and years from now. This role is both strategic and hands-on. You’ll lead a small, talented team of level designers while remaining deeply connected to the craft—jumping into the editor, guiding design decisions, and helping ship meaningful updates and reworks to a live, competitive game. You’ll report to the Director, Game Design and work at the heart of VALORANT’s gameplay evolution. Responsibilities: Set and evolve the design vision for VALORANT maps, ensuring they uphold the game’s tactical DNA while pushing the genre forward Lead both live map updates and major reworks, building on successes like Breeze and shaping how players engage with maps over time Drive innovation on new maps, exploring new ideas while protecting competitive integrity Manage, mentor, and grow a team of level designers, leveling up their craft through hands-on feedback, coaching, and example Partner closely with Map Art, Lighting, and Production, blending gameplay clarity with visual quality and shipping reliably at high polish Guide projects from early concept through final release, balancing player value, development cost, and long-term sustainability Influence senior leadership on product direction, strategy, and priorities related to maps and gameplay experiences Anticipate player needs across platforms, understanding how different audiences engage with competitive shooters Champion player empathy, using data, feedback, and intuition to evolve maps in ways that resonate with the community Required Qualifications: 5+ years experience in a Level Design role; experience as a game design owner of multiple large projects in parallel with successful delivery 1+ years people management experience; experience with mentorship of other designers leading to a promotion or substantial positive improvements in their work Experience creating systemic change to a design team’s process or principles to improve output significantly across the team Experience leading cross-discipline teams to deliver on design goals Experience communicating and expressing complex and nuanced designs in simple ways Leadership experience resolving conflict, and when needed offering difficult feedback with respect Desired Qualifications: Experience working as a developer on an FPS title. apiJob.title: Manager, 3D Character Art - Teamfight Tactics
apiJob.description:
ArtManager, 3D Character Art - Teamfight TacticsSingaporeJob Id: REQ-0009606Riot Games was established in 2006 by entrepreneurial gamers who believe that player-focused game development can result in great games. In 2009, Riot released its debut title League of Legends to critical and player acclaim. As the most played PC game in the world, over 100 million play every month. Players form the foundation of our community and it’s for them that we continue to evolve and improve the League of Legends experience. We’re looking for humble but ambitious, razor-sharp professionals who can teach us a thing or two. We promise to return the favor. Like us, you take play seriously; you’re passionate about games. We embrace those who see things differently, aren’t afraid to experiment, and who have a healthy disregard for constraints. That's where you come in. As an 3D Character Lead in the Singapore Studio Teamfight Tactics Cosmetic team, you will channel your knowledge of color and light to explore and produce the best player value for Teamfight Tactics players. You’ll guide the exploration and execution of the 3D discipline across Character and Environment content. In addition to providing clear direction, you’ll implement the sleek, dynamic and dramatic visual effects that add atmosphere and depth to the gameplay. Through your expertise, you’ll mentor and level-up the craft and game developer skills of your 3D team. You will work with the Associate Art Director to implement a 3D style that fits within the art pillars and punctuates the game design. You will implement the effects and set the high quality bar. You’ll expand and support a high-performing external team and give them guidance and knowledge. You will set high expectations, push for growth for both each individual artist and the entire team, and streamline the production efficiency. You will use your broad knowledge of art, craft, technique, and people management, to set clear goals for your team and make sure the process of delivering those goals is as smooth as possible for your team and their stakeholders. You will ensure your team stays motivated and empowered to take ownership of creativity, you will coach your team to be a triple A team. You will work closely with Product Leads, Delivery Leads, Stakeholders, and the Associate Art Director to stay aligned on vision and priorities. Quickly mediate and resolve craft disagreements and misalignments. Prioritize the craft’s health and support, above personally contributing to art production. Run and manage healthy and productive craft review sessions. Work closely with the others 3D Leads on Teamfight Tactics Cosmetic to together cultivate a great cohseive culture and ensure a consistent quality and style. Responsibilities: As a 3D lead on the team, you will oversee and be accountable for the quality of the work the team delivers and set the bar for excellence. Provide craft direction for your team. Provide as-needed support of all aspects of 3D production process; from set-up through production and troubleshooting You will balance visual satisfaction and gameplay clarity. Provide hands on craft support to the product where needed Support the Associate Art Director on emergent tasks where needed. You will build a world-class team by sourcing AAA 3D artists in China and developing and growing their craft skills and soft skills through training and feedback Build and maintain a healthy 3D team. Set clear vision, provide consistent feedback, and build trust. You will establish and maintain a highly engaged, creative, and motivated culture to unleash artists’ potential. You will drive and improve the rituals in your team to support high-quality craft delivery and enable efficient growth of your team. You will collaborate with Rioters in Singapore and worldwide to make sure product goals are fulfilled, and working practices are appropriate. You will be vigilantly finding creative and effective solutions to eliminate complex problems. Required Qualifications: 5+ years professional experience in the video games industry in 3D. 2+ years of management experience of leading/mentoring teams and individuals. Advanced skills in 3D/2D Software - Maya, 3DS Max, Photoshop Experience creating high-quality, stylized 3D models and textures. Expertise in handpainted stylized. Shipped titles as a Senior or Lead 3D Artist High proficiency in 3D production development, including fundamentals such as timing, shape language, composition, design, shader and color theory A clear understanding of the Teamfight Tactics art style Desired Qualifications: AAA Feature/Game experience Teamfight Tactics community connections/knowledge Experience giving direction and delivering clear, constructive feedback to vendors Past leadership or management experience Development experience creating materials and PBR assets within modern game engines (Unreal, Unity, etc) Broad experience of collaborating with other art disciplines, working production end to end Well-versed in 3D work design for themed and cosmetic content across a variety of game titles, with the ability to form and articulate thoughtful creative insights. Tools experience: have worked in several tools, pipelines, and game engines Fluent in Mandarin Chinese and English, with the ability to communicate effectively in multilingual, cross-cultural treatments Experience managing distributed teams across time zones and in remote work environments Our Perks Full relocation support Full health insurance for you, your spouse, and children Open paid time off Retirement benefits with company matching Life insurance, parental leave, plus short-term and long-term disability Play Fund so you can broaden and deepen your knowledge of our players and community through games We will double down on your donations of time and money to non-profits Apply

apiJob.title: Principal UX Writer - Riftbound
apiJob.description:
DesignPrincipal UX Writer - RiftboundMercer Island, USAJob Id: REQ-0009652As a UX Writer, you’ll shape the player experience across one of our games - from text and tutorials to UI and packaging. Your job isn’t to balance mechanics; it’s to design understanding. You’ll collaborate with designers, producers, and narrative partners to make sure every piece of text feels intuitive, purposeful, and alive in the world of League of Legends. At a high level, this role blends strategic systems thinking and hands-on craft. You’ll define content standards, build editorial systems, and influence game design decisions through clarity, usability, and tone As a Principal UX Writer on Riftbound, you'll help the team scale processes, strengthen voice, and build sustainable ways to keep Riftbound’s world coherent as it grows. You’ll apply UX writing and content design principles to a unique medium - helping transform deep game mechanics into accessible, engaging player experiences. Your work will set the tone and shape how Riftbound communicates, feels, and teaches. This is a hands-on, self-directed role. You’ll manage your own writing pipeline, estimate timelines, track revisions, and coordinate with producers to stay aligned with the release schedule. You won’t manage people, but you’ll manage the process: ensuring nothing slips through cracks and every line meets the clarity and quality standards players deserve. You will report directly to the Game Director. Responsibilities: Write and edit player-facing text, including card names, abilities, tutorials, and in-game UI Collaborate closely with designers to make sure text supports gameplay intent and is easy for players to grasp. You’ll balance clarity and flavor, ensuring every line is both easy to understand and rich with worldbuilding. Define standards and voice - building and maintaining Riftbound’s editorial systems: terminology rules, templates, and voice guidelines , while creating framework that will help everyone, from designers to localization partners, speaking in one consistent, accessible tone Partner with designers to simplify mechanics or adjust copy when complexity risks confusing the player. Collaborate cross-functionally and work closely with design, production, and localization partners to bring language into the development process early. Join playtests, review flows, and offer language insights that make the product more intuitive Build scalable content systems establishing processes for reviewing, documenting, and evolving game text so Riftbound’s world remains clear, cohesive, and future-proof. Manage the content pipeline - helping to scope writing work, align with production on timelines, and prioritize milestones. Brief, edit, and integrate their work to maintain tone and clarity from external writers Flex as cards evolve or new features appear and find smart, concise language solutions under deadlines without losing quality. Model UX writing best practices, collaborate closely with design partners, and advocate for clear, player-first communication across the Riftbound team, helping the team think about writing as design, not decoration. Required Qualifications: 6–10+ years of professional experience in UX writing or editorial roles—ideally with exposure to systems-based content such as games, apps, or other interactive products. Experience owning end-to-end content pipelines - from guidelines to launch. Experience designing content systems. Experience working on TCGs or equivalent genres of game development. Desired Qualifications: Ability to translate complexity into simplicity without losing flavor. Deep experience working cross-functionally with design, production, and art partners. Ability to explain content decisions clearly and give or receive feedback with empathy. Experience with interactive systems. Familiarity with League of Legends For this role, you'll find success through craft expertise and a collaborative spirit that prioritizes the delight of players. We will look at your past studies and experience, but for this role, we also look for dedicated people with a personal relationship with games. If you embody player empathy and care about players' experiences, this is the role for you! Our Perks: Riot focuses on work/life balance, shown by our open paid time off policy and other perks such as flexible work schedules. We offer medical, dental, and life insurance, parental leave for you, your spouse/domestic partner, and children, and a 401k with company match. Check out our benefits pages for more information. At Riot Games, we put players first. That mission drives every decision in our quest to create games and experiences that make it better to be a player. apiJob.description:
ArtSenior 3D Character Artist - Unpublished R&D Product (Contract)Shanghai, ChinaJob Id: 5916作为拳头的驻场3D艺术家，参与项目的3D角色制作，我们一起创建的视觉表现和资产将给玩家带来惊喜和愉悦。 您将与团队的交付经理、设计师、工程师、其他学科艺术家和艺术主管密切合作，以在愿景和优先事项上保持一致。作为3D工艺学科的一部分，您将帮助3D角色的制作与最终材质效果调整，以丰富游戏的氛围和深度，并配合项目的功能需求，制作所需要的功能物件。凭借您的艺术和技术专业知识，您将创建令人惊叹的视觉效果，同时传达重要的游戏信息。您在资产转换和系统设置方面的技能将使您能够重新构想现有元素并创造与玩家产生共鸣的新鲜视觉体验。 职责： 角色制作 负责游戏中高精度风格化角色的制作，确保角色视觉风格与项目整体调性高度统一。 具有一定的手绘能力。 有完整风格化角色项目经验（如二次元、欧美卡通等特定方向）； 熟悉动画绑定基础或特效配合流程； 对流行文化、影视/游戏美术趋势敏感，能快速捕捉风格化设计方向。 熟知《英雄联盟》角色背景设定，能通过概念设计的造型、色彩、服饰细节等传递角色性格与叙事信息传达到制作中。 外包资源审核与管理 外包美术资源的流程质量把控，制定明确的审核标准与反馈机制，高效协调供应商修改并跟进交付进度，确保最终成果符合项目需求； 引擎适配与优化 熟练运用 UE5 引擎 完成角色资产的导入、材质调试、灯光适配及场景整合，确保角色在引擎中的表现效果与设计稿一致（如光照适配、LOD优化等）； 针对不同平台（PC/主机/移动端）输出适配性调整方案，平衡视觉效果与性能表现。 团队协作与技术支持 与绑定、动画、特效等跨职能团队紧密沟通，确保角色设计符合玩法逻辑与叙事需求； 岗位要求： 工作经验5年以上 熟悉风格化NPR和PBR流程制作 具备较好的审美，了解人体解剖学，形态，和色彩理论 熟练掌握建模软件Maya/ 3D Max， 雕刻软件Zbrush，贴图制作软件Substance，以及UE引擎使用 需要有资深的手绘经验 加分项： 前期制作开发经验 一款以上游戏项目上线经验 熟悉《英雄联盟》IP及风格 有一款以上主流游戏的深度体验经验 简历需提供高质量的风格化角色资产作品 在提交申请时，请提供包含高质量艺术作品（包括风格化作品）的作品集，你也有可能被要求完成艺术测试。 对于这个岗位，你将通过工艺专业知识、协作精神以及优先考虑玩家乐趣的决策来获得成功。我们将关注您过去的学习、经验以及您与游戏的个人关系。如果您体现了玩家的同理心并关心玩家的体验，这可能就是您的角色！ 用工性质：与专业人事服务公司FESCO Adecco签订劳动合同.Apply

apiJob.description:
Brand ManagementSenior Brand Manager - TFTShanghai, ChinaJob Id: REQ-0009654Riot Games was established in 2006 by entrepreneurial gamers who believe that player-focused game development can result in great games. In 2009, Riot released its debut title League of Legends to critical and player acclaim. As the most played video game in the world, over 100 million play every month. Players form the foundation of our community and it’s for them that we continue to evolve and improve the League of Legends experience. We’re looking for humble but ambitious, razor-sharp professionals who can teach us a thing or two. We promise to return the favor. Like us, you take play seriously; you’re passionate about games. We embrace those who see things differently, aren’t afraid to experiment, and who have a healthy disregard for constraints. That's where you come in. As Senior Brand Manager – TFT, you will support the development and execution of brand and publishing strategies in China in partnership with Tencent. You’ll collaborate with internal Riot teams and external partners to help ensure campaigns and player-facing efforts resonate with local audiences while aligning with global brand direction. While you won’t own the overall brand strategy, you’ll contribute to its implementation by coordinating reviews, collecting insights, and facilitating alignment between Riot and Tencent. Your role will help ensure that Riot’s strategy games maintain strong brand presence and player connection in the Chinese market. Responsibilities: Brand Strategy Support Lead the development of localized brand strategies by providing input and coordinating with Riot and Tencent stakeholders Help review Tencent campaign plans and creative work, ensuring alignment with brand guidelines and Riot’s values Assist in the documentation and communication of brand narratives and publishing plans for internal and external stakeholders Publishing Collaboration Serve as a point of contact for Tencent’s publishing and marketing teams, offering feedback and support during campaign planning and execution Coordinate with Riot global teams (product, creative, publishing) to communicate China-specific priorities and requests Support the preparation of strategic updates and campaign evaluations based on Tencent and market feedback Player and Market Insights Gather and share insights on the Chinese TFT player communities, including preferences, cultural context, and engagement trends Collaborate with insights teams to support research initiatives and apply findings to publishing decisions Monitor industry trends and competitor activity to help inform brand adjustments Cross-functional Coordination Work closely with Riot China and global publishing teams to align messaging and player-facing efforts Ensure campaign materials and communications reflect consistent tone and positioning Support initiatives that build long-term player trust and brand familiarity in China Required Qualifications: 5+ years of experience in brand management, publishing marketing, or marketing coordination within gaming, entertainment, or digital media Experience collaborating with partners or agencies to support marketing or publishing initiatives Familiarity with gaming audiences, especially in strategy, autobattler, or competitive genres Fluent in Mandarin Chinese and English, with strong communication and coordination skills Demonstrated ability to work cross-functionally and manage multiple stakeholders Solid understanding of Chinese gaming culture and player expectations Desired Qualifications: Experience working with or supporting campaigns involving major publishers like Tencent Knowledge of Riot’s IP and values, especially with a player-first mindset Exposure to live service games and publishing pipelines Passion for strategy games on PC and mobile platforms Apply
